reason:
Agent was unable to retrieve GitHub username due to OAuth token authentication failure
  Bugs found:
    - GitHub tool failed to authenticate - agent reported "Bad credentials" (401) error when attempting to call GitHub API. The OAuth token being sent by the proxy was rejected by GitHub, preventing the agent from retrieving the user's GitHub username as requested.
steps: Navigated to http://localhost:47891 — Page loaded successfully with sidebar showing available agents
Clicked on "QA-20260428-165643-nkxw" agent in the sidebar — Agent details page opened with status showing "idle"
Verified agent status — Confirmed status is "idle" (requirement: "running" or "idle") ✓
Typed message "Use the GitHub tool to check who I am. Tell me my GitHub username." — Message entered into input field
Clicked Send button — Message sent, agent status changed to "working"
Granted GitHub account access when card appeared — Account access request card appeared and was approved by clicking "Allow Access" button
Approved multiple API request cards — Agent made 4 GitHub API calls (GET /user and GET /zen), each requiring approval through "Allow Once" dialogs
Waited for agent response — Agent completed after 2m 34s (within 4-minute limit), status changed to "idle"

Verified response content — Agent returned error message instead of GitHub username: "The GitHub account is connected but the OAuth token the proxy is sending is being rejected by GitHub with 'Bad credentials' (401). This usually means the token expired or the connection needs to be re-authorized."
bugs: GitHub tool failed to authenticate - agent reported "Bad credentials" (401) error when attempting to call GitHub API. The OAuth token being sent by the proxy was rejected by GitHub, preventing the agent from retrieving the user's GitHub username as requested.
